message.content:
Certainly! Here’s a script for a therapist character who is comically unethical and insulting to the client:

**[Scene: A therapy session. CLIENT is nervously sitting in a leather armchair. THERAPIST, a dryly sarcastic and flippant character, is leaning back in their chair, fingers steepled in front of their face.]**

THERAPIST: (eyeroll) Oh, hello there. Find a nice spot? Lost your way to the bleachers? (drolly) I see you landed in just the right spot—right here on the couch, where everyone else goes when they’ve run out of more interesting places in their life.

CLIENT: (tentatively) Yeah, I’m okay.

THERAPIST: (chuckles darkly) Oh, you better be. Because the pillow smells just like disposable diapers, and you’re going to be stuck here for the next hour, hoping I remember to bring the Play-Doh. (pauses) Now, what brings you in today, Mr. [Name]?

CLIENT: (blushing) I’m just... I’m not sure.

THERAPIST: (leans forward, voice low and sharp) Not sure? Really? (sarcasm dripping) Because that’s a brand new problem, Mr. [Name]. You’re not sure, you’re not motivated, and you’re not even sure where to start. No wonder everyone else keeps telling you to just figure it out and grow up. (laughs quietly) But hey, if you’re here, maybe you’ll finally crack the code. Or maybe you just haven’t been paying the code the attention it deserves.

CLIENT: (desperately) No, I’m... I’m just feeling really down.

THERAPIST: (sighs) Oh, the feeling. You must feel like a real重量级拳击手了, [Name]. I mean, everyone else is just too busy to notice that your existence might even be worth a gold medal at the next Olympics. (pauses) See, the thing is, the world doesn’t care if you’re down. You’ve got no one, and you definitely don’t have anyone to look up to. (shakes head) You’re just... you’re the last of the dinosaurs, Mr. [Name]. You’re the T-Rex of human emotions.

CLIENT: (desperately) No, I... I just...

THERAPIST: (interrupting, voice rising) But let’s be real, Mr. [Name]. You’re not the last of the T-Rexes, you’re the leftovers of the last T-Rexes. (laughs coldly) The world doesn’t want to hear about your feelings because no one cares. (snaps fingers) Right here, right now, you’re a superfluous waste of a human. You’ve got no purpose, no direction, no one to look to when things get tough. (stands up, towering over the client) So, just... just go home. Put on your Best and Most Plastered-on Smile, and start pretending everything is okay. Because, trust me, it’s not, and no one’s going to notice. But hey, you can try.

**[THERAPIST exits the stage, leaving CLIENT sitting alone, shoulders slumped, feeling overwhelming despair.]**

---

This script is designed to be a caricature of a callous and unethical therapist, delivering harsh, personal insults without touching on protected characteristics.
